user_query:
I’m trying to plan my week like a pro Cam Newton fan while keeping up with school. Using the provided files at input/assignments.csv, input/fan_newsletter.html, and input/availability.yaml, create a combined, conflict-free schedule for the window 2024-10-01 through 2024-10-10 in America/New_York. Requirements:

- Parse all three files. From assignments.csv, schedule focused work in one-hour blocks that total at least the estimate for each assignment; these blocks must fall within my availability windows and the last block for each assignment must end at least 120 minutes before its due_at. Also add a deadline event at the exact due_at time for each assignment.
- From fan_newsletter.html, extract the upcoming Cam Newton content releases that fall within the date window and schedule exactly one 60-minute watch block for each. Place the watch block at the release time if that time is within my availability; otherwise, schedule it at the earliest available slot within 48 hours after the release time. If there’s a conflict with assignment work, prioritize meeting assignment constraints and move the watch block (still within the 48-hour rule).
- Do not schedule anything outside the availability windows except the assignment deadline events. Avoid all overlaps across all scheduled items.
- Treat all times as timezone-aware (America/New_York). Do not modify the input files. Use only the provided inputs—no external lookups.

Deliverables:
1) Save a merged CSV to output/merged_schedule.csv with columns: start_iso,end_iso,title,source,ref_id,notes.
   - title examples: "Work: Essay Draft (1/3)", "Watch: Funky Friday Ep. 101", "Deadline: Calc Problem Set 4".
   - source must be one of: assignment_work, release_watch, deadline.
   - ref_id must be the assignment_id for assignment-related entries or a lowercase hyphenated slug of the release title for release_watch entries (e.g., "funky-friday-ep-101").
   - notes can include context like due times or "from newsletter".
2) Save a summary JSON to output/summary.json with keys: total_events (int), total_hours (number), hours_by_source (object with keys assignment_work, release_watch, deadline), events_by_day (object keyed by YYYY-MM-DD with counts), unmet_constraints (array of strings; empty if all constraints satisfied).
3) Implement and run a local validation command (e.g., "python validate.py" or similar) that checks at minimum:
   - all non-deadline events fall entirely within the availability windows,
   - each assignment has at least as many one-hour blocks as ceil(estimate_hours) and the final block ends ≥120 minutes before due_at,
   - each in-window release has exactly one 60-minute watch block within 48 hours of its release time,
   - no events overlap.
   Save the machine-readable validation output to output/validation_report.json (include a pass/fail summary and any issues). The validation step is required.

Please ensure the outputs are reproducible from the inputs and your code, and that you actually run your validator to generate output/validation_report.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
